name: Texas Cowpoke Pintos
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Ground Garlic or 4 Pods Crushed Fresh Garlic
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the pinto beans thoroughly and discard any damaged beans or rocks.
2. Place the beans in a pot and cover with water. Bring to a boil, then drain.
3. Return the beans to the pot and add enough fresh water to cover about 2 to 3 inches (or up to 2 inches for a thicker sauce).
4. Add all remaining ingredients except salt. Stir well.
5. If cooking in a crockpot, set it to low heat and cook for 8-10 hours. On the stove, bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook for about 6 hours, stirring occasionally until tender.
6. Season with salt during the last hour of cooking if desired. Serve hot or cold.
This recipe makes approximately 10 servings. It can be frozen for later use in refried beans.
